title: 📖 Story: Carlos's Weekend Trip
description: A story about a weekend trip, told using the near future, preterite, and imperfect tenses.
storyIntro:
You've learned the near future (ir + a), the preterite tense for completed past actions, and the imperfect tense for describing ongoing past situations.

Now let's follow Carlos on a weekend trip and see how all three tenses work together to tell a story!

sentences.spanish: Carlos iba a visitar a su abuela el fin de semana.
sentences.english: Carlos was going to visit his grandmother on the weekend.
On Saturday the weather was nice.

sentences.spanish: Carlos se levantó temprano y se duchó.
sentences.english: Carlos got up early and showered.

sentences.spanish: Él desayunó café con leche y pan.
sentences.english: He had coffee with milk and bread for breakfast.

sentences.spanish: Salió de su casa a las nueve.
sentences.english: He left his house at nine.

sentences.spanish: La abuela vivía en un pueblo pequeño.
sentences.english: The grandmother lived in a small town.

sentences.spanish: Cuando llegó, la abuela estaba en el jardín.
sentences.english: When he arrived, the grandmother was in the garden.

sentences.spanish: Ella estaba contenta. Le gustaba mucho la visita de Carlos.
sentences.english: She was happy. She really liked Carlos's visit.

sentences.spanish: Comieron juntos. La comida estaba muy rica.
sentences.english: They ate together. The food was very tasty.

sentences.spanish: Después, Carlos y su abuela caminaron por el pueblo.
sentences.english: Afterwards, Carlos and his grandmother walked through the town.

sentences.spanish: Carlos le dijo: "Voy a volver pronto."
sentences.english: Carlos told her: "I am going to come back soon."

sentences.spanish: La abuela sonrió y le contestó: "Hasta pronto, mi nieto."
sentences.english: The grandmother smiled and answered: "See you soon, my grandson."

nieto.mnemonic: Think 'knee-to' — a grandchild at your knee.

pronto.mnemonic: Same as English 'pronto' — hurry up, soon!
